Class APISteps

java.lang.Object
io.github.sentinel.steps.APISteps
public class APISteps extends Object

    Description
    static void
    addCookie(String name, String value)
    Adds a cookie to the next request.

    Adds header into API request

    Sets a query string parameter.
    static void
    allowUntrustedSslCertificates()
    Configures the next request to trust all SSL certificates.

    Adds the parsed string keys and values in Configuration for later use
    static void
    loadRequestBody(String testdataName)
    Loads the indicated testdata located in the API object yaml file to use as the json for the body of the request.

    Logs the last request as a curl command at INFO level.
    static void
    logLastResponse()
    Logs the full last response at INFO level.

    sendRequest(String apiCallType, String endpoint)
    Sends a DELETE, GET, POST or PUT request to the specified endpoint.
    static void
    sendRequest(String apiCallType, String parameter, String endpoint)
    Sends a GET or DELETE to the specified endpoint for the indicated record.
    static void

    Loads an API based on the environment you are currently testing.
    static void

    Sets an API key header for the current request.

    Sets Basic authentication credentials for the current request.

    Sets a Bearer token for the current request.

    Sets the body of the active API call to the string passed.

    Sets the retry policy for the next request.

    Stores a value extracted from the response JSON into a named configuration variable for use in subsequent steps.

    Asserts that a JSON array at the given path has the expected number of items.

    Asserts that a response field contains (or does not contain) the expected substring.

    Asserts that a response field equals (or does not equal) the expected value.

    Asserts that a response header contains (or does not contain) the expected substring.

    Asserts that a response header equals (or does not equal) the expected value.

  • Field Details

    • log

      private static final org.apache.logging.log4j.Logger log

  • Constructor Details

    • APISteps

      public APISteps()

  • Method Details

    • setAPI

      @Given("^I use the API named (.*?)$") public static void setAPI(String apiName)
      Loads an API based on the environment you are currently testing. Refer to the documentation in the sentinel.example project for more information.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I use the API named Agify API
      • I use the API named My API

      Parameters:
      apiName - name of the API object we want to use

    • setRequestBody

      @Given("I set the request body to") public static void setRequestBody(String body)
      Sets the body of the active API call to the string passed.
      Parameters:
      body - String the json to be passed as the body of the request.

    • setRequestBodyToMultipartFormDataForFileUpload

      @Given("^I set the request body to upload a file from the location (.*?) as a multipart/form-data with the name (.*?)") public static void setRequestBodyToMultipartFormDataForFileUpload(String fileToUploadPath, String multipartSegmentName) throws FileNotFoundException
      Throws:
      FileNotFoundException

    • loadRequestBody

      @Given("^I load (.*?) to use as the request body$") public static void loadRequestBody(String testdataName)
      Loads the indicated testdata located in the API object yaml file to use as the json for the body of the request.
      Parameters:
      testdataName - String the name of the testdata entry to use

    • addParameter

      @When("^I add an? (.*?) parameter with the value (.*?)$") public static void addParameter(String parameter, String value)
      Sets a query string parameter.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I add a status parameter with the value available
      • I add a name parameter with the value Bob
      • I add an address parameter with the value 143 Down Street

      Parameters:
      parameter - String the parameter to set
      value - String the value to set it

    • sendRequest

      @When("^I send a (DELETE|GET|HEAD|OPTIONS|PATCH|POST|PUT) request to the (.*?) endpoint$") public static void sendRequest(String apiCallType, String endpoint)
      Sends a DELETE, GET, POST or PUT request to the specified endpoint.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I send a GET request to the pet/findByStatus endpoint
      • I send a POST request to the users endpoint
      • I send a PUT request to the amdins endpoint

      Parameters:
      apiCallType -
      endpoint -

    • sendRequest

      @When("^I (DELETE|GET) record (.*) from the (.*?) endpoint$") public static void sendRequest(String apiCallType, String parameter, String endpoint)
      Sends a GET or DELETE to the specified endpoint for the indicated record.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I GET record 10 from the pet endpoint
      • I DELETE record bob from the user endpoint

      Parameters:
      apiCallType - String the type of call to make
      parameter - String the value to send to the endpoint
      endpoint - String the endpoint name as referenced in the swagger file

    • verifyResponseCodeEquals

      @When("^I verify the response code equals (\\d{3})$") public static void verifyResponseCodeEquals(int statusCode)
      Verify that the response code returned from the last API call is what we expect. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status
      Parameters:
      statusCode - int the status code expected

    • verifyResponseTime

      @When("^I verify the response was received in less than (\\d{1,2}(?:[.,]\\d{1,4})?) seconds?$") public static void verifyResponseTime(double time)

    • verifyResponseContains

      @Then("^I validate the response( does not)? (has|have|contains?) the text \"([^\"]*)\"$") public static void verifyResponseContains(String assertion, String matchType, String text)
      Validates text in an API response.
      Parameters:
      assertion - String null to see if the text exists, "does not" to see if it is absent
      matchType - String use "contains" for a partial match otherwise it will be an exact match
      text - String the text to match

    • addHeader

      @When("^I add an? (.*?) header with the value (.*?)$") public static void addHeader(String name, String value)
      Adds header into API request
      Parameters:
      name - String name of a header
      value - String value of the header

    • iInitializeTheData

      @When("I initialize the configuration values as follows") public void iInitializeTheData(String values)
      Adds the parsed string keys and values in Configuration for later use
      Parameters:
      values - String the string with keys and values Example: When I initialize the configuration values as follows """ id: 10 category_name: puppies """

    • setBasicAuthentication

      @Given("^I set basic authentication with username (.*?) and password (.*?)$") public static void setBasicAuthentication(String username, String password)
      Sets Basic authentication credentials for the current request.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I set basic authentication with username admin and password secret
      Parameters:
      username - String the username
      password - String the password

    • setBearerToken

      @Given("^I set the bearer token to (.*?)$") public static void setBearerToken(String token)
      Sets a Bearer token for the current request.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I set the bearer token to eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9
      Parameters:
      token - String the bearer token value

    • setApiKeyHeader

      @Given("^I set the (.*?) API key header to (.*?)$") public static void setApiKeyHeader(String key, String value)
      Sets an API key header for the current request.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I set the X-API-Key API key header to abc123
      Parameters:
      key - String the header name
      value - String the header value

    • storeResponseField

      @Then("^I store the response field \"(.*?)\" as \"(.*?)\"$") public static void storeResponseField(String jsonPath, String variableName)
      Stores a value extracted from the response JSON into a named configuration variable for use in subsequent steps.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I store the response field "$.id" as "petId"
      Parameters:
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression
      variableName - String the configuration variable name to store the value under

    • verifyResponseFieldEquals

      @Then("^the response field \"(.*?)\" should( not)? equal \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseFieldEquals(String jsonPath, String negation, String expected)
      Asserts that a response field equals (or does not equal) the expected value.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response field "$.status" should equal "available"
      • the response field "$.status" should not equal "sold"
      Parameters:
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression
      negation - String null for positive assertion, " not" for negative
      expected - String the expected value

    • verifyResponseFieldContains

      @Then("^the response field \"(.*?)\" should( not)? contain \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseFieldContains(String jsonPath, String negation, String expected)
      Asserts that a response field contains (or does not contain) the expected substring.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response field "$.name" should contain "dog"
      Parameters:
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression
      negation - String null for positive assertion, " not" for negative
      expected - String the expected substring

    • verifyResponseArraySize

      @Then("^the response should have (\\d+) items at \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseArraySize(int expectedCount, String jsonPath)
      Asserts that a JSON array at the given path has the expected number of items.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response should have 3 items at "$.pets"
      Parameters:
      expectedCount - int the expected array size
      jsonPath - String the JSONPath expression targeting an array

    • verifyResponseHeaderEquals

      @Then("^the response header \"(.*?)\" should( not)? equal \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseHeaderEquals(String headerName, String negation, String expected)
      Asserts that a response header equals (or does not equal) the expected value.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response header "Content-Type" should equal "application/json"
      Parameters:
      headerName - String the header name
      negation - String null for positive assertion, " not" for negative
      expected - String the expected value

    • verifyResponseHeaderContains

      @Then("^the response header \"(.*?)\" should( not)? contain \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseHeaderContains(String headerName, String negation, String expected)
      Asserts that a response header contains (or does not contain) the expected substring.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response header "Content-Type" should contain "json"
      Parameters:
      headerName - String the header name
      negation - String null for positive assertion, " not" for negative
      expected - String the expected substring

    • validateResponseSchema

      @Then("^I validate the response matches the schema \"(.*?)\"$") public static void validateResponseSchema(String schemaFilePath)
      Validates the last response against a JSON Schema file located in test resources.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I validate the response matches the schema "schemas/pet.json"
      Parameters:
      schemaFilePath - String the path to the schema file relative to the classpath root

    • logLastRequestAsCurl

      @Then("^I log the last request as a curl command$") public static void logLastRequestAsCurl()
      Logs the last request as a curl command at INFO level.

      Gherkin Example:

      • I log the last request as a curl command

    • logLastResponse

      @Then("^I log the last response$") public static void logLastResponse()
      Logs the full last response at INFO level.

      Gherkin Example:

      • I log the last response

    • setRequestRetry

      @Given("^I set the request to retry up to (\\d+) times with a (\\d+) millisecond delay$") public static void setRequestRetry(int maxRetries, int delayMs)
      Sets the retry policy for the next request.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I set the request to retry up to 3 times with a 500 millisecond delay
      Parameters:
      maxRetries - int the maximum number of retry attempts
      delayMs - int the delay between retries in milliseconds

    • allowUntrustedSslCertificates

      @Given("^I allow untrusted SSL certificates$") public static void allowUntrustedSslCertificates()
      Configures the next request to trust all SSL certificates.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I allow untrusted SSL certificates

    • addCookie

      @Given("^I add a cookie named \"(.*?)\" with the value \"(.*?)\"$") public static void addCookie(String name, String value)
      Adds a cookie to the next request.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • I add a cookie named "session" with the value "abc123"
      Parameters:
      name - String the cookie name
      value - String the cookie value

    • verifyResponseCookieEquals

      @Then("^the response cookie \"(.*?)\" should( not)? equal \"(.*?)\"$") public static void verifyResponseCookieEquals(String cookieName, String negation, String expected)
      Asserts that a response cookie equals (or does not equal) the expected value.

      Gherkin Examples:

      • the response cookie "session" should equal "abc123"
      Parameters:
      cookieName - String the cookie name
      negation - String null for positive assertion, " not" for negative
      expected - String the expected value
